title: T10 Fleet: Cross-machine HALT broadcast
prompt: The factory kill switch (engine/halt.py, flag file engine/store/HALT per docs/kill-switch-spec.md) is per-machine: halting on the M4 Mini does nothing to the dark-factory clone on Roamy (ssh alias macbook-pro-m4, 100.89.32.35, user davidcruwys, clone at /Users/davidcruwys/dev/ad/apps/dark-factory — verified reachable and on clean main at authoring, but its engine predates halt.py). Build the fleet broadcast WITHOUT any Switchboard dependency (that state plane is a deferred fork): (1) git pull --ff-only Roamy's clone so its engine code checks HALT, (2) add engine/fleet.json listing roamy only (fleet scope decision Q8), (3) extend engine/halt.py with a --fleet flag on halt/resume/status that after the LOCAL action (always first, never rolled back on remote failure) broadcasts by direct SSH file write/remove of the remote engine/store/HALT (BatchMode, ConnectTimeout 8, per-machine OK/FAIL report lines, exit 1 if any remote failed), byte-identical behavior without the flag, reader functions is_halted/halt_info untouched, (4) prove a live round-trip (halt --fleet -> flag present on both machines and Roamy's own halt.py reports halted -> resume --fleet -> flag absent on both) captured to engine/store/results/wg-t10-02-roundtrip.txt, (5) update docs/kill-switch-spec.md sections 2 and 6 to match, commit + push only the four named paths, and re-pull Roamy. Never leave a test HALT flag behind on either machine. Read and execute the war game at backlog/wargames/T10-02-fleet-halt-broadcast.md. Follow its moves in order; on any failure signal apply the counter-move; on any abort condition stop and write engine/store/needs-decision/ per the war game, leaving the ticket in running/.
verify: From dark-factory root: engine/fleet.json parses with exactly one machine named 'roamy' whose store ends in engine/store; python3 engine/halt.py status (no flag) still prints 'RUNNING -- no HALT file present.' and is_halted()/halt_info() import and behave unchanged; a live round-trip works — halt --fleet prints 'roamy: HALTED' and ssh macbook-pro-m4 shows the remote HALT payload carrying the reason plus an 'origin' field and Roamy's own engine/halt.py status --json says halted true, then resume --fleet prints 'roamy: RESUMED' and the flag is absent on BOTH machines afterward; docs/kill-switch-spec.md mentions fleet.json; engine/store/results/wg-t10-02-roundtrip.txt is non-empty and committed with a wg-t10-02 commit pushed to origin/main; Roamy's clone fast-forwarded so its engine/halt.py contains the fleet code. Negative: git ls-files shows no HALT/BACKOFF/WAKE-ARMED tracked; the wg-t10-02 commit touches none of engine/orchestrator.py, warm_pool.py, wake.py, status.py or bin/; fleet.json still lists roamy only.
